checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.what: Pauses execution for human review. The run enters a 'paused' state until a human approves, rejects, or redirects.
checkpoint.when: Quality gates, approval workflows, human-in-the-loop decisions, compliance reviews.
checkpoint.config:
type: checkpoint
config:
  prompt: "Review the results and approve to continue."
tips: Creates a checkpoint record in storage with status 'planned'
Resolve via POST /api/runs/{run_id}/checkpoint with decision: approve|reject|redirect
The prompt text is displayed to the reviewer

agent.title: Agent
agent.what: Executes a single registered agent function. The agent receives the current work item state and returns output that is merged into state under output_key.
agent.when: Any step that needs computation: classification, analysis, transformation, API calls.
agent.config:
type: agent
config:
  agent: my_agent_name
  output_key: result_key
tips: Register agents with roots.register_agent(name, callable)
Agent receives {'work_item_state': {...}} as input
Return value is stored at state[output_key]

agent_pool.title: Agent Pool
agent_pool.what: Executes multiple agents in parallel or sequentially, then aggregates their outputs into a single result.
agent_pool.when: Validation batteries, multi-source enrichment, ensemble analysis where multiple agents contribute to one result.
agent_pool.config:
type: agent_pool
config:
  agents: [agent_a, agent_b, agent_c]
  execution_mode: parallel
  aggregation: merge_all
  output_key: combined_result
tips: execution_mode: parallel | sequential | first_pass
aggregation: merge_all deep-merges all agent outputs
All agents receive the same work item state

decision.title: Decision
decision.what: Evaluates conditions and routes execution to the matching edge target. Supports deterministic (expression-based) and AI-powered modes.
decision.when: Branching logic, quality gates, routing based on computed values.
decision.config:
type: decision
config:
  mode: deterministic
  edges:
    - target: pass_node
      condition: "score >= 0.7"
      label: Pass
    - target: fail_node
      condition: "score < 0.7"
      label: Fail
tips: Deterministic mode uses simpleeval for safe expression evaluation
Conditions reference work item state keys directly
AI modes (ai_bounded, ai_checkpoint, ai_autonomous) use LLM for routing

fork.title: Fork
fork.what: Splits execution into parallel branches. Each outbound edge becomes an independent execution path that runs concurrently.
fork.when: Parallel analysis, concurrent data processing, any time work can be split and recombined.
fork.config:
type: fork
config: {}

# Edges define the branches:
edges:
  - from: my_fork
    to: branch_a
  - from: my_fork
    to: branch_b
tips: Must be paired with a join node via fork_join_map
Each branch gets a copy of the current state
Branches run concurrently with asyncio.gather

join.title: Join
join.what: Waits for all forked branches to complete and merges their results back into a single state.
join.when: After a fork node — collects and combines results from parallel branches.
join.config:
type: join
config:
  merge_strategy: merge_all

# Also requires fork_join_map at process level:
fork_join_map:
  my_fork: my_join
tips: merge_all: deep-merges all branch states
collect: gathers branch results into a list under collect_key
allow_partial: true continues even if some branches fail

emit.title: Emit
emit.what: Fires a custom event to the event system. Useful for notifications, logging, and integrating with external systems via webhooks.
emit.when: Notifications, audit trails, triggering external workflows, progress updates.
emit.config:
type: emit
config:
  event_type: "my.custom_event"
  payload_keys:
    - result_a
    - result_b
tips: Events are delivered to all registered event sinks
payload_keys selects which state keys to include in the event payload
Webhooks receive events as HTTP POST with JSON body

end.title: End
end.what: Marks the process run as completed or failed. Terminal node — no outbound edges.
end.when: Every process must have at least one end node. Use status: completed for success, status: failed for failure paths.
end.config:
type: end
config:
  status: completed  # or 'failed'
tips: A process can have multiple end nodes for different outcomes
The run's final status matches the end node's status
After reaching an end node, the run cannot be resumed

retry.title: Retry (Node Feature)
retry.what: Automatically retries failed agent or agent_pool nodes. Configurable backoff strategy, max attempts, and exhaustion behavior.
retry.when: Transient failures, flaky API calls, rate-limited services, any agent that may intermittently fail.
retry.config:
- id: my_agent
  type: agent
  config:
    agent: flaky_service
    output_key: result
  retry:
    max_attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    backoff_seconds: 1.0
    on_exhaustion: fail
tips: Retry is a node-level feature, not a separate node type
backoff strategies: fixed, linear, exponential
on_exhaustion: fail (default) stops the run; route sends to fallback_edge
